literature-report-plan.name: R9-platform-engineering-paas

metadata.title: Platform Engineering and PaaS for Scientific ML: From Infrastructure-as-Code to Infrastructure-as-Platform
metadata.priority: P2 — long-term vision (1-2 years), not publication gate

literature-report-plan.invocation-prompt:
create literature report on "Platform Engineering and PaaS for Scientific ML: From Infrastructure-as-Code to Infrastructure-as-Platform"
Focus: How to build a highly flexible MLOps platform on top of MONAI that is easy to use by researchers who want to do science without understanding infrastructure provisioning. The "infrastructure-as-platform" vision wrapping SkyPilot, Pulumi, Docker, Prefect into a self-service experience.
THREE converging threads:
A) PLATFORM ENGINEERING FOR ML: Internal developer platforms (IDPs) for ML teams. Self-service infrastructure. The maturity from "scripts" to "platform." Infrastructure Platform Engineering (IPE) vs developer-focused platform engineering. Why infrastructure must come first.
B) SCIENTIFIC PAAS: Platform-as-a-Service specifically for scientific computing. Research compute platforms. Galaxy, Nextflow, Snakemake as precedents. How clinical/biomedical research differs from general-purpose cloud. Privacy, reproducibility, audit trail requirements.
C) AI-NATIVE PLATFORMS: The emerging pattern of AI-native PaaS where the LLM IS the interface. Natural language → infrastructure provisioning. Anthropic's Antspace/Baku pattern (environment-manager, Firecracker VMs, full vertical integration from model to runtime). How this changes the DevEx equation for researchers. CopilotKit/AG-UI as the researcher-facing layer.
Context: NEUROVEX already wraps SkyPilot (compute), Pulumi (IaC), Docker (execution), Prefect (orchestration), MLflow (tracking). The question is: how to make this stack invisible to the researcher while maintaining the reproducibility and audit guarantees? This is a P2 vision report, not an immediate implementation plan.
EXCLUDED topics: - Agentic AI details (report 5) - Computational reproducibility fundamentals (R1) - FDA/regulatory compliance details (R2) - Segmentation methods (R3) - Microscopy hardware (R4)
Repo context: /home/petteri/Dropbox/github-personal/minivess-mlops KG domains to update: infrastructure, cloud, architecture Manuscript section: Discussion (future: platform vision)

seed-papers.paper: Gentele (2025). "Platform Engineering Is Failing — Here's Why Infrastructure Comes First." TheNewStack. https://thenewstack.io/platform-engineering-is-failing-heres-why-infrastructure-comes-first/
Bianchessi (2025). "Platform Engineering's True Value Is in Application Development." TheNewStack. https://thenewstack.io/platform-engineerings-true-value-is-in-application-development/
Kreuzberger et al. (2023). "MLOps: Overview, Definition, and Architecture." IEEE Access. https://doi.org/10.1109/ACCESS.2023.3262138
Vieira et al. (2024). "An intelligent framework for managing healthcare big data." Computers in Biology and Medicine. https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0010482524017335
Barrak et al. (2026). "An empirical guide to MLOps adoption." Information and Software Technology. https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0950584926000315
Ren et al. (2025). "A Survey of LLM-based Scientific Agents." arXiv:2506.21072. https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.21072
Afgan et al. (2018). "The Galaxy platform for accessible, reproducible and collaborative biomedical analyses." Nucleic Acids Research. https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6002019/
Di Tommaso et al. (2017). "Nextflow enables reproducible computational workflows." Nature Biotechnology 35, 316-319. https://doi.org/10.1038/nbt.3820
Goecks et al. (2010). "Galaxy: a comprehensive approach for supporting accessible, reproducible computational research." Genome Biology. https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC3832998/
Moore et al. (2023). "OME-Zarr." Histochemistry and Cell Biology. https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s00418-023-02209-1
Glatard et al. (2013). "Reproducibility of neuroimaging analyses across operating systems." IEEE eScience. https://www.computer.org/csdl/proceedings-article/e-science/2013/06683911/12OmNvzJGci
Gruening et al. (2018). "Practical computational reproducibility in life sciences." Cell Systems. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cels.2018.03.014
Wolstencroft et al. (2013). "The Taverna workflow suite." Nucleic Acids Research. https://doi.org/10.1093/nar/gkt328

search-domains.domain: platform engineering internal developer platform ML 2024 2025 2026
infrastructure platform engineering IPE Kubernetes ML workloads
scientific computing PaaS biomedical research platform 2024 2025

literature-report-plan.antspace-context:
Anthropic's internal "Antspace" platform (discovered via reverse engineering of Claude Code's environment-manager binary by @AprilNEA, March 2026): - Go binary (27MB, unstripped) at github.com/anthropics/anthropic/api-go/environment-manager - Firecracker microVM-based execution (KVM hypervisor) - Full deployment protocol: create → upload dist.tar.gz → stream NDJSON status - Powers "Baku" — claude.ai web app builder - Natural language → Claude builds app → deploys to Antspace - AI-native PaaS with vertical integration from LLM to runtime
This is the emerging pattern that NEUROVEX could adopt: researcher describes experiment in natural language → platform provisions GPU compute, configures training, deploys model — all invisible to the researcher.
